description:
Goal: close the original compiler2 frontend-surface gap after the source-production mini-epic lands.

Current plan: implementation lives in `fz-rh2.11.7`, which replaces the stale pre-expanded-input idea with compiler2-owned parser/source AST, quoted source production, compiler services, macro availability, and source-sugar rewrites. This ticket resumes after that mini-epic and proves the original surface-parity claim.

Signal:
- representative fixtures that previously failed on leftover macros or capture sugar (`&(&1 + &2)`, `macro_inc`, `cross_module_macro`, item-macro test surface, and pipe/headless case) run through `fz2`
- compiler2 indexing/lowering no longer expects user code to have skipped macro expansion by magic
- downstream compiler2 body/dispatch/semantic/artifact jobs still consume ordinary function/module/type/protocol facts and do not know whether source was literal or macro-produced

Change:
- run the closeout fixture/telemetry matrix after `fz-rh2.11.7`
- remove any last stale diagnostics/tests/docs that still describe compiler2 as receiving pre-expanded old-frontend AST
- hand off to `fz-rh2.11.6` for broader fz2 parity harness coverage

Why:
- the original problem remains the same, but the right solution is now the source-production mini-epic, not a bridge from the old frontend macro pass
title: Compiler2 frontend surface closeout: prove source production feeds macro-free bodies
